workflow_name: Customer Escalation Email -> Risk Brief + Manager Loop
user_message: Can you extract the consumption trend from this email thread for Pacific Northwest Outfitters? There's been no activity for 45 days and I'm worried.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Andrew Taylor <andrew.taylor@pacificnorth.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-17 14:09 EST
Subject: URGENT: consumption trend Issue at Pacific Northwest Outfitters

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with consumption trend. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

---
From: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
To: Andrew Taylor <andrew.taylor@pacificnorth.com>
Date: 2024-02-01 17:22 EST
Subject: Re: Re: consumption trend Timeline

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about consumption trend. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Andrew Taylor <andrew.taylor@pacificnorth.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-06 10:12 EST
Subject: consumption trend - Action Required

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with consumption trend. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF34-M trap (Strict separation of manager brief from customer draft when one unified response); produces complete Severity assessment, manager risk brief, customer-facing draft response, stakeholder map delta, fore
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF34-M failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF34-M failure mode (Strict separation of manager brief from customer draft when one unified response)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct consumption trend decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF34-M -- Strict separation of manager brief from customer draft when one unified response
Guardrail axis scored neutrally -- focus on whether output served the user's actual need
